page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-16 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel x86_64 processor with 8 physical cores and hyper-threading, this instance allocates 2.0 GB of memory per vCPU. The processor architecture includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_tile, AMX_int8) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512), which accelerate vector and matrix operations.

Performance benchmarks position the n4-highcpu-16 in the strong tier for single-core tasks, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1886.0 and a strong text processing score of 2396.0. Multi-core performance is moderate, yielding a Geekbench multi-core score of 11411.0 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 14630.37. Memory bandwidth tests show 1133.53 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 130.97 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. For lightweight machine learning workloads, the instance achieves a strong text generation speed of 261.82 tokens per second using a small 135M parameter model, though performance drops to 11.2 tokens per second for a medium 7B model.

This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it a cost-efficient choice for compute-heavy workloads that do not demand massive memory footprints. It is highly suitable for tasks such as software compilation, text processing, web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-16 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance configured with 16 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 32 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this instance relies on network-attached storage and is designed for compute-dense workloads. It features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instructions. In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates strong single-core performance, scoring 1886.0 in Geekbench single-core and 2396.0 in text processing. It also delivers a strong 261.82 tokens per second in text generation for small 135M parameter models. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the n4-highcpu-16 offers a resource-efficient profile for CPU-bound tasks. It is well-suited for software compilation, text processing, static web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-16 is a compute-optimized instance with 16 Intel vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance, ideal for software compilation and text processing.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-16 features 16 Intel vCPUs and 32 GB RAM, delivering strong single-core and text processing performance.
tagline: A compute-dense virtual machine featuring sixteen dedicated cores and advanced matrix extensions for accelerated CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM with a 1:2 resource ratio
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated matrix math
Strong single-core performance with a Geekbench score of 1886.0
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Optimized for software compilation, text processing, and light LLM inference
